critique.summary: Function 'render_form' has 1 critical issue(s) (security) requiring immediate remediation before deployment.

wpcs_compliance.issue: WPCS coding style is acceptable in 'render_form' but lacks PHPDoc completeness
wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.issue: No direct database queries in 'render_form' (source: advanced-custom-fields.json) — SQL safety dimension acceptable
sql_safety.fix: If database access is added later, always use $wpdb->prepare() with proper placeholders

security.issue: 'render_form' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests
security.fix: Add wp_verify_nonce($_POST['_wpnonce'], 'action_name') before processing any user input; use wp_nonce_field() in forms

performance.issue: 'render_form' performs potentially expensive operations in a loop without caching (source: advanced-custom-fields.json)
performance.fix: Cache expensive computed values using wp_cache_set()/wp_cache_get() with appropriate cache group and expiry

wp_api_usage.issue: 'render_form' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: advanced-custom-fields.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit

dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs
